evals.prompt: I have a receiving order with expected barcodes and quantities. Make my MatrixScan Count app scan against that list and show progress. File: IntegratedActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a List of TargetBarcode via TargetBarcode.create(data, quantity), wraps them in a BarcodeCountCaptureList via BarcodeCountCaptureList.create(listener, targets), applies it with barcodeCount.setBarcodeCountCaptureList(...), and implements BarcodeCountCaptureListListener to observe progress (onCaptureListSessionUpdated / onCaptureListCompleted). The progress bar shows automatically.

assertions.text: Each expected barcode is created with TargetBarcode.create(data, quantity) — the static factory, not a constructor
A BarcodeCountCaptureList is created with BarcodeCountCaptureList.create(listener, targets) where targets is a List<TargetBarcode>
A BarcodeCountCaptureListListener is implemented (onCaptureListSessionUpdated and/or onCaptureListCompleted), reading session.correctBarcodes / wrongBarcodes / missingBarcodes
It does not hand-roll progress tracking when the built-in capture list provides it; no guessed API like setCaptureList(...) (the Android method is setBarcodeCountCaptureList)

evals.prompt: When scanning against my list in MatrixScan Count, let the user accept or reject barcodes that aren't on the list. File: IntegratedListScanningActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the not-in-list action by creating a BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings, setting enabled = true, and assigning it to barcodeCountView.barcodeNotInListActionSettings. Accepted/rejected barcodes then appear in the capture-list session.

assertions.text: A BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings is constructed and its enabled property is set to true
It does not invent a settings flag on BarcodeCountSettings for this — the action settings live on the view

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count receiving flow, automatically stop scanning once every expected barcode on the list has been counted. File: IntegratedListScanningActivity.kt
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.disableModeWhenCaptureListCompleted = true on the BarcodeCountSettings before constructing the BarcodeCount mode.

assertions.text: settings.disableModeWhenCaptureListCompleted = true is set on the BarcodeCountSettings
It is set before BarcodeCount.forDataCaptureContext(...) is called
It uses the real property disableModeWhenCaptureListCompleted — not a guessed API
